title: Doctor William Gorgas riding the Panama Railroad
creator: Apollo Pictures
description: Film has ca. 2 ft. of views of Dr. William Crawford Gorgas, chief sanitation officer of the Panama Canal (1904-1913) and member of the Isthmian Canal Commission, standing in front of a building; location of this sequence is undetermined. Remainder of film shows Dr. Gorgas and an unidentified man riding on a Panama Canal Company train. The two men are silhouetted against passing scenery of the Canal Zone as Dr. Gorgas shows the other man points of interest. The train passes a body of water which is probably a part of the canal, countryside, buildings probably on Front Street, Colon, including a YMCA club; final scene of people walking across tracks after the train passes.
subject: Historical figures; Canal construction; Train trip
source: Library of Congress
rights: The Library of Congress is not aware of any U.S. copyright or other restrictions in the vast majority of motion pictures in these collections. Absent any such restrictions, these materials are free to use and reuse.

title: Panama Station with Monumento a los Bomberos
creator: Wilde, Bradley (Donor)
description: The monument in the center of the square is called the Monumento a los Bomberos (Firefighters Monument), also commonly referred to as the Monumento a la Tragedia de El Polvorín (Memorial to the El Polvorín Tragedy). The monument was inaugurated on May 5, 1916. It was unveiled by the President of Panama, Belisario Porras, exactly two years after the tragic explosion of the "El Polvorín" ammunition depot. The creation of this monument is what officially established and gave the name to the public square we know today as Plaza 5 de Mayo.
subject: Station
source: Panama Canal Museum Collection at the University of Florida
rights: Staff believe that the item is out of copyright and in the Public Domain under U.S. Copyright Law, but a determination was not made as to its copyright status under the laws of other countries.

title: "New" Gatun Station
creator:
McDaniel (Creator)
Wilde, Bradley (Donor)
description: The Old Gatún Station was inundated and relocated during the creation of Gatun Lake and the Panama Canal. A brand new, permanent Gatún Train Station was completed on January 27, 1909, serving the newly relocated "New Town" of Gatún. The station was built on higher ground east of the Gatun Locks, complete with a two-story commissary next to the tracks. Includes several US Navy sailors in the crowd.

title: Gamboa Bridge
creator: Wilde, Bradley (Donor)
description: Railway tracks are on the Gamboa bridge. The bridge spans the Chagres River right at the point where it flows into the Panama Canal's Gatun Lake.  While the old bridge remains an iconic historic landmark, a newer, wider two-lane concrete bridge was constructed directly alongside it to accommodate modern traffic entering the town.
